briefdescription.para: The constructor of the Bond object.

detaileddescription.para:
This function will call Init to do initialization.
dim
the dimenstion of the Bond
bd_type
the type (see bondType) of the bond
Init(const cytnx_uint64 &dim, const bondType &bd_type)

memberdef.argsstring: (const bondType &bd_type, const std::vector< std::vector< cytnx_int64 > > &in_qnums, const std::vector< cytnx_uint64 > &degs, const std::vector< Symmetry > &in_syms={})
memberdef.name: Bond

briefdescription.para: The constructor of the Bond object.

detaileddescription.para: This function will call Init to do initialization.

parameterdescription.para: the type (see bondType) of the bond
input the quantum numbers of the bond (for symmetry case)
the degrees of freedom of the bond. A zero degeneracy represents an empty sector while retaining its quantum number and the Bond's symmetry metadata.
input the symmetries of the bond (for symmetry case)

simplesect.para: This function is can only use for symmetry case.

memberdef.argsstring: (const bondType &bd_type, const std::initializer_list< std::vector< cytnx_int64 > > &in_qnums, const std::vector< cytnx_uint64 > &degs, const std::vector< Symmetry > &in_syms={})
memberdef.name: Bond

memberdef.argsstring: (const bondType &bd_type, const std::vector< cytnx::Qs > &in_qnums, const std::vector< cytnx_uint64 > &degs, const std::vector< Symmetry > &in_syms={})
memberdef.name: Bond

memberdef.argsstring: (const bondType &bd_type, const std::vector< std::pair< std::vector< cytnx_int64 >, cytnx_uint64 > > &in_qnums_dims, const std::vector< Symmetry > &in_syms={})
memberdef.name: Bond

param.type: const std::vector< std::pair< std::vector< cytnx_int64 >, cytnx_uint64 > > &

briefdescription.para: init a bond object

detaileddescription.para:
dim
the dimension of the bond (rank)
bd_type
the tag of the bond, it can be BD_BRA, BD_KET as physical tagged; or BD_REG as regular bond (rank)
details:
each bond can be tagged with BD_BRA or BD_KET that represent the bond is defined in Bra space or Ket space. @pre 1. \p dim may be 0 for an empty vector space.
The bond can be tagged with bondType.BD_BRA or bondType.BD_KET, or bondType.BD_REG depending on the usage.

para.verbatim:
Dim = 10 |type: REGULAR
Dim = 10 |type: KET>
Dim = 4 |type: KET> U1:: +0 +2 -1 +3 Deg>> 1 1 1 1
Dim = 4 |type: <BRA U1:: +0 +2 -1 +3 Z2:: +0 +1 +1 +0 Deg>> 1 1 1 1
para.heading: python API:

para.verbatim:
Dim = 10 |type: REGULAR
Dim = 10 |type: KET>
Dim = 4 |type: KET> U1:: +0 +2 -1 +3 Deg>> 1 1 1 1
Dim = 4 |type: <BRA U1:: +0 +2 -1 +3 Z2:: +0 +1 +1 +0 Deg>> 1 1 1 1

memberdef.argsstring: (const bondType &bd_type, const std::vector< std::vector< cytnx_int64 > > &in_qnums, const std::vector< cytnx_uint64 > &degs, const std::vector< Symmetry > &in_syms={})
memberdef.name: Init

briefdescription.para: init a bond object

detaileddescription.para:
This function is initialization for symmetry bond case.
each bond can be tagged with BD_BRA or BD_KET that represent the bond is defined in Bra space or Ket space.
the bond can have arbitrary multiple symmetries, with the type of each symmetry associate to the qnums are provided with the in_syms.

parameterdescription.para: the tag of the bond, it can be bondType.BD_BRA, bondType.BD_KET as physical tagged and cannot be bondType.BD_REG (regular bond).
the quantum number(s) of the bond. it should be a 2d vector with shape (# of unique qnum labels, # of symmetry).
the degeneracy correspond to each qunatum number sets specified in the qnums, the size should match the # of rows of passed-in qnums.
a vector of symmetry objects of the bond, the size should match the # of cols of passed-in qnums. [Note] if qnums are provided, the default symmetry type is Symmetry::U1

listitem.para:
The size of degs need to same as the size of in_qnums.
in_qnums and degs cannot be empty.
All sub-vector in in_qnums MUST have the same size.
If in_syms is provided, the size of in_syms MUST same as the size of the sub-vector in in_qnums.
bd_type cannot be bondType.BD_REG.
The quantum numbers in in_qnums and in_syms need to be consistent. For example, you cannot set the quntum number 2 in Z(2) symmetry.
If quantum number(s) are provided (which means the bond is with symmetry) then the bond MUST be tagged with either bondType.BD_BRA or bondType.BD_KET.
If the bond is non-symmetry, then it can be tagged with bondType.BD_BRA or bondType.BD_KET, or bondType.BD_REG depending on the usage.
The "bond dimension" is the sum over all numbers specified in degs.

memberdef.argsstring: (const bondType &bd_type, const std::vector< std::pair< std::vector< cytnx_int64 >, cytnx_uint64 > > &in_qnums_dims, const std::vector< Symmetry > &in_syms={})
memberdef.name: Init

param.type: const std::vector< std::pair< std::vector< cytnx_int64 >, cytnx_uint64 > > &

para.ref: Init(const bondType &bd_type, const std::vector<std::vector<cytnx_int64>> &in_qnums, const std::vector<cytnx_uint64> &degs, const std::vector<Symmetry> &in_syms)

briefdescription.para: return the current bond type (see cytnx::bondType).
return the current quantum number set(s) by reference

simplesect.para: [2d vector] with shape: (dim, # of Symmetry)
Compare to qnums_clone(), this function return reference.

briefdescription.para: return the clone (deep copy) of the current quantum number set(s)

simplesect.para: [2d vector] with shape: (dim, # of Symmetry)
Compare to qnums(), this function return the clone (deep copy).

briefdescription.para: return the dimension of the bond

simplesect.para: [cytnx_uint64]

briefdescription.para: return the number of the symmetries

simplesect.para: [cytnx_uint32]

briefdescription.para: return the vector of symmetry objects by reference.

simplesect.para: Compare to syms_clone() const, this function return by reference.
[vector of Symmetry]

briefdescription.para: return copy of the vector of symmetry objects.

simplesect.para: Compare to syms() const, this function return the clone (deep copy).
[vector of Symmetry]

briefdescription.para: change the tag-type of the instance Bond

parameterdescription.para: the new tag-type, it can be bondType.BD_BRA, boncType.BD_KET or bondType.BD_REG. See cytnx::bondType.

simplesect.para: You cannot change the symmetry bond (bondType.BD_BRA or bondType.BD_KET to regular type (bondType.BD_REG) except the size of the quantum number is 0.

briefdescription.para: create a new instance of Bond with type changed to the new tag-type.

parameterdescription.para: the new tag-type, it can be bondType.BD_BRA, bondType.BD_KET or bondType.BD_REG. See cytnx::bondType.

simplesect.para: This is equivalent to Bond.clone().set_type()
You cannot change the symmetry bond (bondType.BD_BRA or bondType.BD_KET to regular type (bondType.BD_REG) except the size of the quantum number is 0.
clone(), set_type(const bondType & new_bondType)

briefdescription.para: create a new instance of Bond with type changed in between bondType.BD_BRA / bondType.BD_KET.
Change the bond type between bondType.BD_BRA and bondType.BD_KET in the Bond.
change the tag-type to the default value bondType.BD_REG.

simplesect.para: The size of quantum number should be 0. Namely, you cannot clear the symmetric bond.

briefdescription.para: return a copy of the instance Bond

simplesect.para: [Bond] a new instance of Bond that have the same contents

para.verbatim:
Dim = 10 |type: REGULAR
True False True True

briefdescription.para: Combine the input bond with self, inplacely.

parameterdescription.para: the bond that to be combined with self.

this parameter is only used when the bond is symmetric bond (bondType.BD_BRA or bondType.BD_KET). If is_grp is true, the basis with duplicated quantum number will be grouped together as a single basis. See group_duplicates(std::vector<cytnx_uint64> &mapper) const.

listitem.para: The type of two bonds (see cytnx::bondType) need to be same.
The Symmetry of two bonds should be same.

simplesect.para: Compare to combineBond(const Bond &bd_in, const bool &is_grp) const, this function in inplace function.

briefdescription.para: combine the input bond with self, and return a new combined Bond instance.

parameterdescription.para: the bond that to be combined.

this parameter is only used when the bond is symmetric bond (bondType.BD_BRA or bondType.BD_KET). If is_grp is true, the basis with duplicated quantum number will be grouped together as a single basis. See group_duplicates(std::vector<cytnx_uint64> &mapper) const.

simplesect.para: [Bond] a new combined bond instance.

listitem.para: The type of two bonds (see cytnx::bondType) need to be same.
The Symmetry of two bonds should be same.

simplesect.para: Compare to combineBond_(const Bond &bd_in, const bool &is_grp), this function will create a new Bond object.

briefdescription.para: combine multiple input bonds with self, and return a new combined Bond instance.

parameterdescription.para: the bonds that to be combined with self.

this parameter is only used when the bond is symmetric bond (bondType.BD_BRA or bondType.BD_KET). If is_grp is true, the basis with duplicated quantum number will be grouped together as a single basis. See group_duplicates(std::vector<cytnx_uint64> &mapper) const.

simplesect.para: [Bond] a new combined bond instance.

listitem.para: The type of all bonds (see cytnx::bondType) need to be same.
The Symmetry of all bonds should be same.

simplesect.para: Compare to combineBond_(const std::vector<Bond> &bds, const bool &is_grp), this function will create a new Bond object.

briefdescription.para: combine multiple input bonds with self, inplacely

parameterdescription.para: the bonds that to be combined with self.

this parameter is only used when the bond is symmetric bond (bondType.BD_BRA or bondType.BD_KET). If is_grp is true, the basis with duplicated quantum number will be grouped together as a single basis. See group_duplicates(std::vector<cytnx_uint64> &mapper) const.

listitem.para: The type of all bonds (see cytnx::bondType) need to be same.
The Symmetry of all bonds should be same.

simplesect.para: Compare to combineBond(const std::vector<Bond> &bds, const bool &is_grp), this function will create a new Bond object.

briefdescription.para: combine multiple input bonds with self, and return a new combined Bond instance.

detaileddescription.para:
@deprecated This function is deprecated. Please use \n Bond combineBond(const Bond &bd_in, const bool &is_grp) const instead.
bds
the bonds that to be combined with self.
is_grp
this parameter is only used when the bond is symmetric bond (bondType.BD_BRA or bondType.BD_KET). If is_grp is true, the basis with duplicated quantum number will be grouped together as a single basis. See group_duplicates(std::vector<cytnx_uint64> &mapper) const.
[Bond] a new combined bond instance.
The type of all bonds (see cytnx::bondType) need to be same.
The Symmetry of all bonds should be same.
Compare to combineBonds_(const std::vector<Bond> &bds, const bool &is_grp), this function will create a new Bond object.
combineBonds_(const std::vector<Bond> &bds, const bool &is_grp)
Example:

briefdescription.para: combine multiple input bonds with self, inplacely

detaileddescription.para:
@deprecated This function is deprecated. Please use \n combineBond_(const std::vector<Bond> &bds, const bool &is_grp) instead.
bds
the bonds that to be combined with self.
is_grp
this parameter is only used when the bond is symmetric bond (bondType.BD_BRA or bondType.BD_KET). If is_grp is true, the basis with duplicated quantum number will be grouped together as a single basis. See group_duplicates(std::vector<cytnx_uint64> &mapper) const.
The type of all bonds (see cytnx::bondType) need to be same.
The Symmetry of all bonds should be same.
Compare to combineBonds(const std::vector<Bond> &bds, const bool &is_grp), this function will create a new Bond object.
combineBonds(const std::vector<Bond> &bds, const bool &is_grp)
Example:

briefdescription.para: return a sorted qnum sets by removing all the duplicate qnum sets.

parameterdescription.para: output the counts of the unique quantum numbers.

simplesect.para: The bond cannot be regular type (namely, bondType.BD_REG)
std::vector<std::vector<cytnx_int64>> unique_qnums

briefdescription.para: return a sorted qnum sets by removing all the duplicate qnum sets.

simplesect.para: The bond cannot be regular type (namely, bondType.BD_REG)
std::vector<std::vector<cytnx_int64>> unique_qnums

briefdescription.para: return the degeneracy of specify qnum set.

parameterdescription.para: input the quantum number you want to get the degeneracy.

simplesect.para: the input qnum should match the number of the Symmetry. (see Nsym())

cytnx_uint64 degeneracy @note if the bond has no symmetries, return 0. if the degeneracy queried does not exist, return 0, and the indicies is empty @see getDegeneracy(const std::vector<cytnx_int64> &qnum, std::vector<cytnx_uint64> &indices) const.

briefdescription.para: return the degeneracy of specify qnum set.

parameterdescription.para: input the quantum number you want to get the degeneracy.
output the indices location of the quantum number qnum.

simplesect.para: the input qnum should match the number of the Symmetry. (see Nsym())

cytnx_uint64 degeneracy @note If the bond has no symmetries, return 0. If the degeneracy queried does not exist, return 0, and the indicies is empty. @see getDegeneracy(const std::vector<cytnx_int64> &qnum) const.

briefdescription.para: return all degeneracies.

simplesect.para: std::vector<cytnx_uint64> degeneracy
getDegeneracies() const, getDegeneracy(const std::vector<cytnx_int64> &qnum) const, getDegeneracy(const std::vector<cytnx_int64> &qnum, std::vector<cytnx_uint64> &indices) const.

briefdescription.para: return fermionic parity for a given quantum number

parameterdescription.para: quantum number

simplesect.para: parity of the quantum number; EVEN for bosonic degree of freedom, ODD for fermionic

briefdescription.para: Group the duplicated quantum number, inplacely.

detaileddescription.para:
This function will group the duplicated quantum number and return the mapper, where mapper is about the new index from old index via new_index = return<cytnx_uint64>[old_index].
The Bond need to be symmetric type (namely, bondType should be bondType.BD_BRA or bondType.BD_DET, see bondType.)
Compare to the function group_duplicates(std::vector<cytnx_uint64> &mapper) const, this function is inplace function.
This function will sort ascending of the quantum number.
group_duplicates(std::vector<cytnx_uint64> &mapper) const, has_duplicate_qnums() const.
std::vector<cytnx_uint64> mapper

briefdescription.para: Group the duplicated quantum number and return the new instance of the Bond object.

detaileddescription.para:
This function will group the duplicated quantum number and return the new instance of the Bond object. It will also the mapper, where mapper is about the new index from old index via new_index = return<cytnx_uint64>[old_index].
mapper
the new index from old index via new_index = return<cytnx_uint64>[old_index].
The Bond need to be symmetric type (namely, bondType should be bondType.BD_BRA or bondType.BD_DET, see bondType.)
Compare to the function group_duplicates_(), this function will create the new instance of Bond object.
This function will sort ascending of the quantum number.
group_duplicates_(std::vector<cytnx_uint64> &mapper), has_duplicate_qnums() const.
Bond

briefdescription.para: Check whether there is duplicated quantum numbers in the Bond.

detaileddescription.para:
This function will check whether there is any duplicated quantum number is the Bond. If yes, return ture. Otherwise, return false.
For the regular bond (bondType.BD_REG), it will always return false.
group_duplicates_(std::vector<cytnx_uint64> &mapper) group_duplicates(std::vector<cytnx_uint64> &mapper) const
bool

briefdescription.para: Calculate the reverse of the quantum numbers.

detaileddescription.para:
This function will calculate the reverse of the qunatum numbers by the reverse rule of the Symmetry. See Symmetry.reverse_rule().
You may use this function if the Bra-Ket mismatch.
Symmetry.reverse_rule()
std::vector<std::vector<cytnx_int64>>

briefdescription.para: Save the Bond object to the file.

detaileddescription.para:
Save the Bond object to the file. The file extension will be automatically added as ".cybd".
fname
the file name of the Bond object (exclude the file extension).
Load(const std::string &fname)

simplesect.para: Save(const std::string &fname) const;

briefdescription.para: The comparison operator 'equal to'.

detaileddescription.para:
The comparison operators to compare two Bonds. If two Bond object are same, return true. Otherwise, return false. This equal to operator will compare all the "value" of the Bond object. Even the Bond object are different object (different address), but they are same "value", it will return true.
operator!=(const Bond &rhs) const

briefdescription.para: The comparison operator 'not equal to'.

detaileddescription.para:
The comparison operators to compare two Bonds. More precisely, it is the opposite result of the operator==(const Bond &rhs) const.
operator==(const Bond &rhs) const

briefdescription.para: The multiplication operator of the Bond object.
The multiplication assignment operator of the Bond object.
Load the Bond object from the file.

parameterdescription.para: the file name of the Bond object.

simplesect.para: The file need to be the file of Bond object, which is saved by the function Bond::Save(const std::string &fname) const.

briefdescription.para: the object contains auxiliary properties for each Tensor rank (bond)

detaileddescription.para:
The Bond object is used to construct the bond of the UniTensor.
For non-symmetric UniTensor (regular UniTensor, that means cytnx::UTenType.Dense, see cytnx::UTenType), the bond type need will be set as bondType.BD_REG defaultly. And you can set the bond type as bondType.BD_KET or bondType.BD_BRA if you want to describe the it as ket or bra basis. For non-symmetric case, you cannot input the quantum numbers and Symmetry object.
For symmetric UniTensor (cytnx::UTenType.Block, see cytnx::UTenType), the bond type need to be set as bondType.BD_KET or bondType.BD_BRA depend on what physical system you describe. And you should input the quantum numbers and Symmetry objects.
